intro: Migrating from TypeScript to PHP changes runtime targets from client or Node.js environments to an ecosystem explicitly crafted for server-side web scripting. Developers make this switch to deploy code onto pervasive web infrastructure, interface natively with relational databases, and build dynamic server-rendered page configurations. This transition trades build-step compilation systems and event loops for a stateless request-response lifecycle with robust object-oriented tools.

diffs.from: Static structural type system compiled away before execution, letting developers create elastic structural data layers.
diffs.to: Gradual nominal type framework checked dynamically during runtime execution, supporting strict mode declarations and class properties.

diffs.from: Modern C-style formatting utilizing curly brace frameworks, lexical scoping rules, and arrow functions.
diffs.to: Sigil-prefixed variable structures ($var), arrow operator object access (->), explicit string concats (.), and mandatory class scopes.

diffs.from: Managed engine heap allocation optimized for persistent client interactions and asynchronous event routing.
diffs.to: Isolated, request-scoped memory architecture that allocates and completely destroys all script variables upon serving each web request.

diffs.from: Single-thread non-blocking event runtime driven natively by Promise resolution loops.
diffs.to: Synchronous, thread-per-request execution model; asynchronous operations require specialized extensions (Swoole, Fiber) or queues.

diffs.from: Minimal foundational standard footprint necessitating a complex grid of third-party NPM tool additions.
diffs.to: Enormous array of integrated web-centric functions for database connections, HTML filtering, text parsing, and encryption natively.

examples.title: Class Definitions and Type Annotations
examples.description: Contrasting a basic user tracking model defined across platforms, utilizing modern PHP constructor promotion.
examples.toCode:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

class NetworkUser {
    // Constructor property promotion
    public function __construct(
        public readonly int $id,
        public string $email
    ) {}
}

examples.title: Data Array Dictionary Handling
examples.description: Mapping configuration values using typical object properties versus PHP's optimized associative array models.

tips.title: Activate Strict Typing Always
tips.body: Place 'declare(strict_types=1);' at the absolute top of every PHP file to force runtime type failures on mismatched method arguments.

tips.title: Swap Object References for Associative Arrays
tips.body: Where TypeScript uses unstructured open objects ({}), modern PHP utilizes fast, optimized associative array matrices to store complex dictionary keys.

tips.title: Understand the Stateless Lifecycle
tips.body: PHP state resets completely between client hits. Cache persistent metrics inside shared data stores like Redis instead of global application variables.

tips.title: Transition from NPM to Composer
tips.body: Manage project packages and autoloader paths using Composer, the standard dependency tracking framework for the PHP community.

faqs.question: How are JSON payloads decoded across these development tracks?
faqs.answer: TypeScript uses 'JSON.parse()'. PHP processes strings natively using 'json_decode($json, true)', which translates data blocks into typed associative arrays.

faqs.question: What stands in for TypeScript interface assertions within PHP code configurations?
faqs.answer: PHP supports native structural 'interface' models. Code objects must nominally declare compliance via the 'implements' keyword to pass verification checks.

faqs.question: Can PHP code run persistently like a Node.js web listener?
faqs.answer: Traditionally PHP processes requests via CGI engines like FPM. However, runtimes like Swoole or RoadRunner allow persistent memory execution loops.
